MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-20-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. LYNNE M. COATES OF THE HABBAS, NASSERI & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. 1-916-953-7365 APPERING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. NORA M. NACHTSHEIM OF LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-438-5934 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CAREY LIMOUSINE SF INC.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CAREY INTERNATIONAL, INC., CAREY LIMOUSINE S.F., INC. and EMBARQUE SAN FRANCISCO, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is GRANTED as to Carey International, Inc., Embarque San Francisco, Inc. and denied as to Carey Limousine S.F. Inc. The court grants the motion as to defendants Carey International Inc. and Embarque San Francisco, Inc. The undisputed evidence demonstrates that there is no basis to hold Carey International Inc. and Embarque San Francisco, Inc. vicariously liable for the accident. (Defendants' Statement of Undisputed Facts 4-11; 15-22.). Carey Limousine S.F. Inc. failed to maintain its burden of production. The existence of an employment, agent, or joint venture relationship is generally a factual issue. Carey Limousine S.F. Inc. fails to show that plaintiff Parimah Mehrrostami's claim "cannot be established" within the meaning of CCP 437c(p)(2). Carey Limousine S.F. Inc. cites paragraph 7 of the Galindo declaration to support its assertion that there was no employment, agent, or joint venture relationship. That paragraph is inapposite because it relates only to Carey International Inc. (the parent entity), not Carey Limousine S.F. Inc. Paragraphs 14 and 15 of the Galindo declaration and exhibit I indicate that Mr. Lee did not drive any customers for Carey Limousine S.F. Inc. on the day of the accident. Mr. Lee, however, was unsure regarding who the fare was for and testified that an employee of Carey Limousine S.F. Inc. referred the fare to him. (Lee Deposition, 121:7-121:21; 125:23-126:1.) Carey Limousine S.F. Inc. cites paragraph 19 of the Galindo declaration to assert that Carey Limousine S.F. Inc. did not receive any remuneration from the fare. Plaintiff's objection to this evidence is sustained. Mr. Galindo's foundation for paragraph 19 is lacking as it appears that he is relying on company records for which no foundation has been laid for the business records exception to the hearsay rule. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CCP 437 c(g) and CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/hk

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS MARK SALAZAR and SYNDROME FILE INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - The demurrer to the first cause of action for negligence/personal injury is sustained without leave to amend.
